prompts.prompt: You are a local general-purpose assistant. Evaluate this idea: a personal app that uses local memory, compact context, and MoE routing. Give a decision, risks, and an MVP in English.
Compare three strategies for handling long context in a local assistant: sliding window, summary memory, and semantic retrieval. Choose a hybrid policy and explain the tradeoffs.

Rewrite this message in a clear, concise, and warm tone: 'I am not sure this architecture makes sense. Maybe we are making life harder than necessary, but I want to understand whether it can become a real product.'
Describe a tool-using flow for a local assistant that needs to read documents, remember preferences, and answer without sending sensitive data to the cloud.

candidates.label: Qwen3 1.7B MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Very small fallback candidate for routing, classification, summaries, and cheap compaction.

candidates.label: Qwen3 4B MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Small general fallback with better quality headroom than the 1.7B model.

candidates.label: Gemma 4 E4B it MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Fast local fallback candidate. This artifact is validated with the pinned MLX profile: mlx==0.31.1, mlx-metal==0.31.1, mlx-lm==0.31.2.

candidates.label: Qwen3 30B-A3B Instruct 2507 MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Best first serious resident general expert candidate for a 24 GB Apple Silicon machine.

candidates.label: Gemma 4 26B-A4B it MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Main general alternative; likely stronger for multimodal/productive writing paths.

candidates.label: Gemma 4 26B-A4B it OptiQ MLX 4-bit
candidates.notes: Newer sensitivity-aware quant. Test as stretch because memory headroom is tighter.
